questions.q: The Battles of Tarain were fought between Prithviraj Chauhan and which adversary?
questions.explanation: The battles pitted Prithviraj Chauhan against the Ghurid ruler Muhammad of Ghor.

questions.q: How many battles of Tarain were there?
questions.explanation: Two battles were fought at Tarain, in 1191 and 1192 CE.

questions.q: In which years were the two Battles of Tarain fought?
questions.explanation: The First Battle was in 1191 CE and the Second in 1192 CE.

questions.q: Who won the First Battle of Tarain in 1191?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Chauhan defeated Muhammad of Ghor in the First Battle of Tarain.

questions.q: Who won the Second Battle of Tarain in 1192?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor defeated Prithviraj Chauhan in the decisive Second Battle of Tarain.

questions.q: Tarain is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Tarain, modern Taraori, lies in present-day Haryana, north of Delhi.

questions.q: Muhammad of Ghor came from which region to invade India?
questions.explanation: He was the Ghurid ruler based at Ghor in present-day Afghanistan.

questions.q: The Ghurid victory at the second battle opened the way for which new political order in India?
questions.explanation: The victory led to the establishment of Turkish rule and the founding of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: Prithviraj Chauhan, who fought at Tarain, was a king of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj III was a ruler of the Chahamana (Chauhan) dynasty of Ajmer and Delhi.

questions.q: The Battles of Tarain are remembered as a turning point in the history of which part of India?
questions.explanation: They marked a decisive shift toward Turkish rule in northern India.

questions.q: Muhammad of Ghor's dynasty is known to history as the?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor belonged to the Ghurid dynasty, distinct from the earlier Ghaznavids.

questions.q: In the First Battle of Tarain, Muhammad of Ghor was personally what?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor was wounded and his army routed, compelling his retreat in 1191.

questions.q: A key factor in the Ghurid success at the Second Battle of Tarain was their use of what?
options: Mounted archers and mobile cavalry tactics
questions.explanation: Ghurid mounted archers and swift cavalry manoeuvres outmatched the Rajput formations in 1192.

questions.q: After the Second Battle of Tarain, which general of Muhammad of Ghor later founded the Slave dynasty of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Qutb-ud-din Aibak, the Ghurid general left in charge, founded the Mamluk (Slave) dynasty in 1206.

questions.q: What broadly happened to Prithviraj Chauhan after the Second Battle of Tarain?
options: He converted and joined the Ghurids
questions.explanation: Prithviraj was captured following the second battle and was put to death.

questions.q: Which earlier raider, distinct from Muhammad of Ghor, had invaded India repeatedly in the 11th century?
questions.explanation: Mahmud of Ghazni raided India in the early 11th century, well before the Ghurid campaigns at Tarain.

questions.q: The Rajput army at Tarain relied heavily on which traditional war animal?
questions.explanation: Rajput armies of the period made heavy use of war elephants alongside cavalry.

questions.q: One reason offered for the Rajput defeat in 1192 was their failure to do what after the 1191 victory?
options: Decisively pursue and finish the Ghurid threat
Sign a treaty with the Cholas
questions.explanation: Prithviraj did not press his 1191 advantage, allowing Muhammad of Ghor to regroup and return stronger.

questions.q: The Battles of Tarain are sometimes also called the Battles of?
questions.explanation: Tarain corresponds to modern Taraori, so the battles are also called the Battles of Taraori.

questions.q: Tarain lies on a strategic route guarding the approach to which city?
questions.explanation: Tarain sat on the historic invasion route from the northwest toward Delhi.

questions.q: Just before Tarain, Muhammad of Ghor had captured which strategic Punjab fortress town?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor seized Bhatinda (Tabarhindah), provoking Prithviraj's march that led to the First Battle of Tarain.

questions.q: The Second Battle of Tarain is widely regarded by historians as what kind of turning point?
options: A decisive shift enabling lasting Muslim rule in north India
questions.explanation: It is seen as a watershed that enabled the durable establishment of Turkish-Muslim rule in northern India.

questions.q: The contemporary chronicler whose work the 'Tabaqat-i-Nasiri' is a key Persian source for Tarain was?
questions.explanation: Minhaj-i-Siraj's Tabaqat-i-Nasiri is an important medieval Persian source on the Ghurid conquests.

questions.q: In the Second Battle of Tarain, Muhammad of Ghor reportedly divided his cavalry into several light divisions to do what?
options: Harass and tire the Rajputs before a final charge
questions.explanation: Ghurid mounted archers used hit-and-run tactics to exhaust the Rajput host before delivering a decisive reserve charge.

questions.q: After Tarain, the Ghurids quickly pushed eastward and defeated which Gahadavala king of Kannauj?
questions.explanation: In 1194 the Ghurids defeated Jaichand of Kannauj at the Battle of Chandawar, extending their conquests.

questions.q: The Ghurid army at Tarain was composed largely of which type of soldiers central to Central Asian warfare?
questions.explanation: Mounted horse archers were the backbone of Ghurid and Central Asian armies, giving them tactical mobility.

questions.q: How is the size of the opposing armies at Tarain best characterised in the sources?
options: Reported figures are large but likely exaggerated
Said to be only a few hundred each
questions.explanation: Medieval chronicles give very large army figures that historians regard as exaggerated and approximate.

questions.q: Following his victory, Muhammad of Ghor returned to his Central Asian base, leaving Indian affairs largely to whom?
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor delegated the administration and further conquest of north India to his general Qutb-ud-din Aibak.

questions.q: The dramatic tale of a blinded Prithviraj slaying Muhammad of Ghor with an arrow is found mainly in which source, regarded as legend?
questions.explanation: This episode appears in the bardic Prithviraj Raso and is treated as legendary rather than historical.

questions.q: Muhammad of Ghor's success at Tarain is often contrasted with his earlier defeat in India at which battle around 1178?
options: The Battle of Kasahrada (against the Chaulukyas of Gujarat)
questions.explanation: Muhammad of Ghor had earlier been defeated by the Chaulukyas (Solankis) of Gujarat at Kasahrada around 1178 CE.
